title: lithocarpus cucullatus
intro: Lithocarpus cucullatus, also known as the Tanbark Oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Lithocarpus cucullatus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune function. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used in the form of tinctures or salves for topical applications.
